---
title: "M3 — Data & Tooling"
toc: false
---

Load, clean, explore, and visualize real data with NumPy, Pandas, and Matplotlib;
run a repeatable EDA; and pick up reproducibility and data ethics *as habits*. The
running dataset is **Palmer Penguins** (CC0), loaded offline — no install, ₹0.

## Start here

Work through the chapters in order, beginning with
[**Chapter 1 — NumPy & vectorization**](ch01-numpy-vectorization.qmd). Each runs in
your browser; press **Run** on every code block. Data loads offline from a vendored
CSV via `lib/data.py`, and plots render with Matplotlib in Pyodide.

| # | Chapter |
|---|---------|
| 1 | [NumPy & vectorization](ch01-numpy-vectorization.qmd) |
| 2 | [Pandas Series & DataFrame](ch02-pandas-series-dataframe.qmd) |
| 3 | [Indexing & selection](ch03-indexing-selection.qmd) |
| 4 | [Cleaning data](ch04-cleaning-data.qmd) |
| 5 | [Joins & groupby](ch05-joins-groupby.qmd) |
| 6 | [Reshaping](ch06-reshaping.qmd) |
| 7 | [Plotting with Matplotlib](ch07-plotting-matplotlib.qmd) |
| 8 | [Statistical plots](ch08-statistical-plots.qmd) |
| 9 | [The EDA workflow](ch09-eda-workflow.qmd) |
| 10 | [Scaling & encoding (a preview)](ch10-scaling-encoding.qmd) |
| 11 | [Notebooks vs scripts](ch11-notebooks-vs-scripts.qmd) |
| 12 | [Environments & reproducibility](ch12-environments-reproducibility.qmd) |
| 13 | [Data ethics (a preview)](ch13-data-ethics.qmd) |
| 14 | [Messy real-world data](ch14-messy-real-world-data.qmd) |

## Module quiz

When you've finished the chapters, test the whole module. Your score is saved in
your browser.

{{< quiz module-quiz.yml >}}

## Capstone

Finish with the module project:
[**End-to-end EDA**](capstone.md) — run a full exploratory data analysis on a *new*
public dataset (a different one from the penguins you used here, to test transfer):
load → clean → answer at least three questions with plots **and** numbers → write up
findings, with an ethics note on the data's source and license.
